content.problemStatement:
In this problem you will be given a sequence of triples of positive integers. For example:

 

`   1  2   5
   5  2   6
   1  3   8
   8  1  11
   1  1   6
  10  1   6
  11  3   6
  10  4   8
  10  1  11
` 

Given a pair of numbers A and B, a chain connecting A and B is a sequence of triples

A0W0A1,A1W1A2,A2W2A3,...Ak‚àí2Wk‚àí2Ak‚àí1,Ak‚àí1Wk‚àí1AkA_0 W_0 A_1, \quad A_1 W_1 A_2,  \quad A_2 W_2 A_3,  \quad ... A_{k-2} W_{k-2} A_{k-1}, \quad  A_{k-1} W_{k-1} A_kA0‚ÄãW0‚ÄãA1‚Äã,A1‚ÄãW1‚ÄãA2‚Äã,A2‚ÄãW2‚ÄãA3‚Äã,...Ak‚àí2‚ÄãWk‚àí2‚ÄãAk‚àí1‚Äã,Ak‚àí1‚ÄãWk‚àí1‚ÄãAk‚Äã

such that


A0=AA_0 = AA0‚Äã=A
Ak=BA_k = BAk‚Äã=B
For each iii, 0‚â§i<k0 \leq i < k0‚â§i<k, either the triple AiA_iAi‚Äã WiW_iWi‚Äã Ai+1A_{i+1}Ai+1‚Äã or the triple Ai+1A_{i+1}Ai+1‚Äã WiW_iWi‚Äã AiA_iAi‚Äã is in the given set of triples.

The value of such a chain is the sum of the WiW_iWi‚Äãs in the chain. For example, here is a chain connecting 111 and 111111 using the triples listed above:

1‚ÄÖ‚Ää1‚ÄÖ‚Ää6,6‚ÄÖ‚Ää3‚ÄÖ‚Ää111 \; 1 \; 6, \quad 6 \; 3 \; 11116,6311

The value of this chain is 1+3=41+3 = 41+3=4.

Here is another chain connecting 111 and 111111.

1‚ÄÖ‚Ää1‚ÄÖ‚Ää6,6‚ÄÖ‚Ää1‚ÄÖ‚Ää10,10‚ÄÖ‚Ää1‚ÄÖ‚Ää111 \; 1 \; 6, \quad 6 \; 1 \; 10, \quad 10 \; 1 \; 11116,6110,10111

The value of this chain is 1+1+1=31+1+1 = 31+1+1=3. You can verify that among all chains connecting 111 and 111111 this is the one with least value.

Sometimes there may be no chains connecting the given pair of numbers. For example, there is no chain connecting 111 and 222.

You will be given a sequence of triples and a pair of numbers. Your task is to find the value of the least value chain connecting the two numbers.

Input:
The first line of the input has three numbers MMM, AAA and BBB, where MMM is the number of triples, and your task is to find the value of the least value chain connecting AAA and BBB.
The next MMM lines (lines 2,3,‚Ä¶,M+12,3, \ldots,M+12,3,‚Ä¶,M+1) describe the triples. Line i+1i+1i+1 contains the three positive integers XiX_iXi‚Äã, YiY_iYi‚Äã and ZiZ_iZi‚Äã that make up the ithi^{th}ith triple.
Output:
If there is at least one chain connecting AAA and BBB the first line of the output must consist of a single word `YES`.
In that case the second line must contain a single integer value indicating the value of the least valued chain connecting AAA and BBB.
If there are no chains connecting AAA and BBB the output should contain a single line with the word `NO` on it.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved using **Dijkstra‚Äôs shortest path algorithm**, which is typically used to find the shortest path in a graph with weighted edges. First, you represent the graph using an adjacency list where each node stores the edges (connections to other nodes) and the corresponding weights. Then, you initialize a **priority queue** to always explore the node with the smallest known path cost first. Starting from the source node, you explore all neighboring nodes, updating the minimum path cost to reach each node. If the destination node can be reached, you return the **minimum cost**; otherwise, you return **‚ÄúNO‚Äù**.
